questions.questionText:
In a hydrogen-like atom, the energy needed to take out the electron from an excited orbit is 3.4 eV.

Find the de-Broglie wavelength (in Å) of the electron:

(Given: radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is 0.53Å)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Minimum amount of time required to measure the kinetic energy of an electron with a speed of $100 \pm 1~\mathrm{ms^{-1}}$ is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Photon having energy equivalent to the binding energy of 4th state of $\mathrm{He}^{+}$atom is used to eject an electron from the metal surface of work function 1.4 eV . If electrons are further accelerated through the potential difference of 4 V then the minimum value of De-broglie wavelength (in Å) associated with the electron is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A proton and a $\mathrm{Li}^{3+}$ nucleus are each given kinetic energy by applying the same accelerating potential. Let $\lambda_{\mathrm{Li}}$ and $\lambda_P$ be the de-Broglie wavelengths of $\mathrm{Li}^{3+}$ and the proton, respectively. Then $\frac{\lambda_{\mathrm{L}^{3+}}}{\lambda_p}$ can be written as $x \times 10^{-1}$.

The value of $x$ is $\_\_\_\_$ . (Rounded off to the nearest integer)

(Mass of $\mathrm{Li}^{3+}=8.3$ mass of proton)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A photon having energy 4.5 eV is incident on a metal surface whose work function is 3.0 eV. If the uncertainty in position is $\frac{25}{4 \pi}$ Å, determine the uncertainty in the measurement of the de-Broglie wavelength (in $Å$).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An accelerated electron has a speed of $5 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ with an uncertainty of $0.02 \%$. The uncertainty in finding its location while in motion is $y \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{~m}$. The value of $y$ is :

[Use mass of electron $=9.1 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{~kg}, h=6.63 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}, \pi=3.14$ ]
